title: Comb Tree — Classification
description:
A binary tree (type: Feuille of int | Noeud of arbre * arbre) is a 'peigne' if all internal nodes (except possibly the root) have at least one leaf child.
A 'peigne strict' also requires the root to have at least one leaf child.
A 'peigne rangé' requires the RIGHT child of every internal node to be a leaf.

Given a binary tree in level-order (integers for leaves, 'I' for internal node, 'N' for null), classify it.

Note: an internal node has two children; a leaf has none.
inputSpec: A single line: level-order. Leaves are integers, internal nodes are 'I', null = 'N'.
outputSpec: RANGE if peigne rangé, STRICT if peigne strict (but not rangé), PEIGNE if peigne (but not strict), OTHER.

botCodes.code:
vals=input().split()
n=len(vals)
def is_leaf(i):return i<n and vals[i]!='I' and vals[i]!='N'
def is_null(i):return i>=n or vals[i]=='N'
def is_internal(i):return i<n and vals[i]=='I'

def check():
    # BFS check
    from collections import deque
    q=deque([0])
    is_range=True;is_strict=True;is_peigne_ok=True
    root_has_leaf=False
    while q:
        i=q.popleft()
        if is_null(i) or is_leaf(i):continue
        l,r=2*i+1,2*i+2
        lf=is_leaf(l);rf=is_leaf(r)
        if i==0:
            if not(lf or rf):is_strict=False
            root_has_leaf=lf or rf
        else:
            if not(lf or rf):is_peigne_ok=False
        if not rf:is_range=False
        if is_internal(l):q.append(l)
        if is_internal(r):q.append(r)
    if not is_peigne_ok:return 'OTHER'
    if is_range:return 'RANGE'
    if is_strict:return 'STRICT'
    return 'PEIGNE'
print(check())
